name: Kaden Groves
bio: Fast Australian sprinter. Crashed out of Stage 1 final sprint.

name: Tobias Bayer
bio: Young Austrian sprinter and sprint support rider.

name: Francesco Busatto
bio: Italian sprinter and leadout specialist. Top-4 finish in Stage 18.

name: Jonas Geens
bio: Belgian breakaway specialist, active in multiple escape attempts.

name: Edward Planckaert
bio: Belgian sprinter from the famous Planckaert cycling family.

name: Jensen Plowright
bio: Young Australian all-rounder in his first Grand Tour.

name: Johan Price-Pejtersen
bio: Tall Danish rider, strong in breakaways and against the clock.

name: Luca Vergallito
bio: Italian climber active in Stage 14 early breakaway.

name: Santiago Buitrago
bio: Colombian climber who withdrew early in the race.

name: Damiano Caruso
bio: Veteran Italian climber, 9th overall through consistency.

name: Fran Miholjevic
bio: Young Croatian all-rounder in his first Grand Tour.

name: Afonso Eulálio
bio: Revelação of the race. Wore pink for 9 stages, finished 6th overall.

name: Mathijs Paasschens
bio: Dutch domestique in service of the team's GC ambitions.

name: Alec Segaert
bio: Won Stage 12 with a late attack from the breakaway.

name: Robert Stannard
bio: Young Australian all-rounder and sprint support.

name: Edoardo Zambanini
bio: Young Italian punchy sprinter. 2nd in Stage 18 behind Magnier.

name: Filippo Magli
bio: Italian climber from the domestic Bardiani team.

name: Martin Marcellusi
bio: Young Italian climber. Active in early breakaways.

name: Luca Paletti
bio: Italian breakaway specialist.

name: Vicente Rojas
bio: Spanish climber in the Italian domestic team.

name: Manuele Tarozzi
bio: Italian sprinter. In early Stage 1 breakaway.

name: Nikita Tsvetkov
bio: Russian all-rounder riding under neutral flag.

name: Filippo Turconi
bio: Young Italian climber in his first Grand Tour.

name: Enrico Zanoncello
bio: Young Italian sprinter. Withdrew during the race.

name: Felix Gall
bio: Best climber in GC group behind Vingegaard. Consistent 2nd on every mountain finish.

name: Tobias Lund Andresen
bio: Tall Danish sprinter. 2nd on Stage 1 in a close sprint finish.

name: Tord Gudmestad
bio: Young Norwegian sprinter. Top-8 finish in Stage 1.

name: Gregor Mühlberger
bio: Experienced Austrian all-rounder and support rider.

name: Oliver Naesen
bio: Belgian classics specialist providing sprint support for Gall.

name: Rasmus Søjberg Pedersen
bio: Young Danish all-rounder in his debut Grand Tour.

name: Callum Scotson
bio: Big Australian rouleur providing power for Gall's GC bid.

name: Johannes Staune-Mittet
bio: Talented young Norwegian climber. Impressive in mountain stages.

name: Alexander Cepeda
bio: Young Ecuadorian climber active in mountain breakaways.

name: Samuele Battistella
bio: Italian puncheur and classics rider.

name: Markel Beloki
bio: Spanish climber in support role.

name: Madis Mikhels
bio: Estonian sprinter. Multiple top-10 sprint finishes including Stage 1 (5th) and Stage 18 (7th).

name: Darren Rafferty
bio: Irish rider in breakaway and support roles.

name: James Shaw
bio: English climber who withdrew during the race.

name: Michael Valgren
bio: Won Stage 17 from breakaway before withdrawing.

name: Jardi van der Lee
bio: Young Dutch climber who featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Rémi Cavagna
bio: Powerful French TT and breakaway specialist.

name: Cyril Barthe
bio: French climber in support role.

name: Axel Huens
bio: Belgian breakaway rider. Active in Stage 20 breakaway group.

name: Johan Jacobs
bio: Swiss rider who excels in breakaway scenarios.

name: Josh Kench
bio: Young New Zealand all-rounder.

name: Paul Penhoët
bio: Young French climber.

name: Rémy Rochas
bio: French climber, 28th in GC after a solid Giro.

name: Brieuc Rolland
bio: Young French climber in his debut Grand Tour.

name: Giulio Ciccone
bio: Takes KOM jersey on Stage 19 by cresting the Passo Giau first (Cima Coppi).

name: Simone Consonni
bio: Italian sprint support and track cyclist.

name: Derek Gee-West
bio: Brilliant Stage 19 performance. 2nd on Queen Stage, moved into GC top 5.

name: Amanuel Ghebreigzabhier
bio: Eritrean climber in support role for Ciccone and Milan.

name: Jonathan Milan
bio: Tall Italian powerhouse sprinter. Multiple top-3 finishes in sprint stages.

name: Matteo Sobrero
bio: Italian TT and puncheur specialist. 26th in GC.

name: Tim Torn Teutenberg
bio: Tall young German sprinter developing his Grand Tour legs.

name: Max Walscheid
bio: Towering German sprinter. 9th on Stage 1, opened the sprint before being outpaced.

name: Arnaud De Lie
bio: Belgian sprinting talent. Withdrew before the race started.

name: Toon Aerts
bio: Belgian cyclocross champion turned road racer.

name: Joshua Giddings
bio: British rider who withdrew during the race.

name: Simone Gualdi
bio: Italian breakaway specialist.

name: Milan Menten
bio: Belgian puncheur who withdrew during the race.

name: Lorenzo Rota
bio: Italian climber and domestique.

name: Jonas Rutsch
bio: Tall German all-rounder.

name: Lennert Van Eetvelt
bio: Talented young Belgian GC climber who had to withdraw.

name: Enric Mas
bio: Movistar's GC leader. 17th overall after struggling to match Vingegaard's mountain pace.

name: Orluis Aular
bio: Venezuelan sprinter. Featured in Stage 18 finale.

name: Iván García Cortina
bio: Tall Spanish classics rider in support of Mas.

name: Juan Pedro López
bio: Spanish climber and former KOM wearer. 22nd overall.

name: Lorenzo Milesi
bio: Young Italian climber featured in Stage 19 Queen Stage group. 19th overall.

name: Nelson Oliveira
bio: Experienced Portuguese road specialist.

name: Javier Romo
bio: Spanish climber who withdrew during the race.

name: Einer Rubio
bio: Colombian climber. Active on all major climbs including Stage 19 Queen Stage. 12th overall.

name: Egan Bernal
bio: Two-time Grand Tour winner. Back in form — 10th overall with a fine Stage 20 result (+1:25).

name: Thymen Arensman
bio: Young Dutch climber. Breakthrough Grand Tour — 4th overall at just 24 years old.

name: Filippo Ganna
bio: Dominant in Stage 10 time trial. World-class specialist on his home race.

name: Jack Haig
bio: Australian climber in support of the team's GC riders. 27th overall.

name: Magnus Sheffield
bio: Talented young American rider — strong TT ability, developing as a Grand Tour rider.

name: Embret Svestad-Bårdseng
bio: Young Norwegian climber active in Stage 19 mountain breakaway group.

name: Connor Swift
bio: English sprinter and support rider.

name: Ben Turner
bio: Tall English rouleur in support role.

name: Alessandro Pinarello
bio: Italian all-rounder.

name: Jan Hirt
bio: Experienced Czech climber. 15th overall after featuring in multiple mountain breakaways.

name: Ryan Mullen
bio: Irish time trial and rouleur specialist.

name: Nick Schultz
bio: Australian climber with Tour de France stage win experience.

name: Dion Smith
bio: New Zealand breakaway specialist.

name: Jake Stewart
bio: English sprinter who withdrew during the race.

name: Corbin Strong
bio: New Zealand sprinter. 5th on Stage 18 in a strong result.

name: Ethan Vernon
bio: English sprinter. Impressive 3rd on Stage 1 before withdrawing.

name: David de la Cruz
bio: Experienced Spanish climber. Active in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Mark Donovan
bio: Young British all-rounder. Featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: David González
bio: Spanish climber in a support role.

name: Chris Harper
bio: Australian climber. Featured in Stage 19 Queen Stage breakaway group.

name: Jai Hindley
bio: 2022 Giro winner. Strong performance to finish 3rd overall at this edition.

name: Giovanni Aleotti
bio: Young Italian climber in support of the team's GC riders.

name: Nico Denz
bio: German breakaway specialist and stage hunter.

name: Gianni Moscon
bio: Italian classics powerhouse.

name: Giulio Pellizzari
bio: One of the revelations of this Giro. 11th overall aged just 21. Active on all major climbs.

name: Mick van Dijke
bio: Young Dutch rouleur in support of Hindley.

name: Aleksandr Vlasov
bio: Russian climber (riding neutral) in support role.

name: Ben Zwiehoff
bio: German domestique in support of the team leaders.

name: Paul Magnier
bio: Only 20 years old. Hat-trick of stage wins and holds the Maglia Ciclamino.

name: Ayco Bastiaens
bio: Belgian sprint support for Magnier.

name: Gianmarco Garofoli
bio: Young Italian climber. Featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Andrea Raccagni Noviero
bio: Young Italian support climber.

name: Jasper Stuyven
bio: Milan-Sanremo winner providing perfect lead-out for Magnier in Stage 18.

name: Fabio Van den Bossche
bio: Young Belgian puncheur developing his Grand Tour legs.

name: Dries Van Gestel
bio: Belgian sprinter. 10th on Stage 1. Sprint support throughout.

name: Filippo Zana
bio: Italian climber and former Giro stage winner.

name: Ben O'Connor
bio: Australian climber, consistent through all mountain stages. 14th overall.

name: Pascal Ackermann
bio: German sprinter. 7th on Stage 1 before withdrawing.

name: Koen Bouwman
bio: Dutch climber and stage hunter. 29th overall.

name: Robert Donaldson
bio: Australian sprint support rider.

name: Felix Engelhardt
bio: Young German climber who withdrew during the race.

name: Alan Hatherly
bio: South African MTB cross-over rider.

name: Christopher Juul-Jensen
bio: Veteran Danish domestique. Featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Warren Barguil
bio: Experienced French climber and former Tour de France stage winner. 21st overall.

name: Sean Flynn
bio: Young Irish all-rounder.

name: Chris Hamilton
bio: Australian climber. 30th in GC after completing all 20 stages to date.

name: Gijs Leemreize
bio: Young Dutch climber. Featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Tim Naberman
bio: Young Dutch all-rounder.

name: Frank van den Broek
bio: Young Dutch sprinter.

name: Casper van Uden
bio: Young Dutch sprinter.

name: Mattia Bais
bio: Italian climber active in Stage 14 breakaway and Stage 18 attack.

name: Ludovico Crescioli
bio: Young Italian climber. Active in Stage 19 and Stage 20 mountain groups.

name: Giovanni Lonardi
bio: Italian sprinter. 6th on Stage 1.

name: Mirco Maestri
bio: Veteran Italian breakaway specialist.

name: Andrea Mifsud
bio: Maltese rider. Featured in Stage 18 early breakaway.

name: Thomas Pesenti
bio: Young Italian all-rounder.

name: Diego Pablo Sevilla
bio: Spanish climber. In Stage 1 breakaway, collected KOM points.

name: Alessandro Tonelli
bio: Veteran Italian domestique. Featured in Stage 14 breakaway.

name: Jonas Vingegaard
bio: Absolutely dominant. 5 stage wins, wore pink from stage 14. One of the great Giro performances.

name: Victor Campenaerts
bio: World Hour Record holder and TT specialist providing crucial support.

name: Wilco Kelderman
bio: Veteran Dutch GC climber who withdrew before the race.

name: Timo Kielich
bio: Young Belgian support rider for Vingegaard.

name: Sepp Kuss
bio: 2023 Vuelta winner. Wins brutal Queen Stage (S19) in a solo attack over the Passo Giau.

name: Bart Lemmen
bio: Young Dutch climber in support of Vingegaard.

name: Davide Piganzoli
bio: Young Italian in Vingegaard's service, still finished 8th overall — remarkable for a debut GT.

name: Tim Rex
bio: Young Australian support rider for Vingegaard.

name: Michael Storer
bio: Consistent on every mountain stage. 7th overall is an excellent result for Tudor.

name: Will Barta
bio: American all-rounder supporting Storer's GC bid.

name: Robin Froidevaux
bio: Young Swiss climber in his debut Grand Tour.

name: Fabian Lienhard
bio: Swiss all-rounder on his home team.

name: Luca Mozzato
bio: Italian sprinter with Grand Tour stage winning experience.

name: Mathys Rondel
bio: Young French climber. Featured in Stage 19 lead group and Stage 20 breakaway.

name: Florian Stork
bio: German rouleur in domestique role.

name: Larry Warbasse
bio: Experienced American climber. In Stage 20 breakaway, last caught 11km from summit.

name: Igor Arrieta
bio: Won a sensational Stage 5 mountain attack. Active on Stage 20 final climb. 25th GC.

name: Mikkel Bjerg
bio: Big Danish TT specialist providing crucial time trial support.

name: Jan Christen
bio: Young Swiss climber. Active in Stage 14 breakaway with Van der Lee.

name: Jhonatan Narváez
bio: Hat-trick of stage wins. Led points classification twice. Withdrew after Stage 17.

name: António Morgado
bio: Young Portuguese climber. Took bonus seconds at Stage 1 intermediate sprint.

name: Dylan Groenewegen
bio: Fast Dutch sprinter. Crashed in Stage 1 finale and missed the sprint. Active in later stages.

name: Harthijs de Vries
bio: Dutch sprint support rider for Groenewegen.

name: Matyas Kopecky
bio: Czech all-rounder.

name: Tomas Kopecky
bio: Czech sprinter.

name: Lukáš Kubis
bio: Slovak sprinter. 10th on Stage 18.

name: Niklas Larsen
bio: Danish track specialist turned road rider.

name: Wout Poels
bio: Veteran Dutch climber. Accelerated in Stage 14 before being dropped. 18th overall.

name: Elmar Reinders
bio: Dutch sprinter in support of Groenewegen.

name: Markus Hoelgaard
bio: Norwegian breakaway specialist.

name: Johannes Kulsett
bio: Young Norwegian breakaway rider. Active in Stage 14 and Stage 18 attacks.

name: Fredrik Dversnes Lavik
bio: Won Stage 15 in Milan when the peloton underestimated the breakaway.

name: Andreas Leknessund
bio: Versatile Norwegian. Challenged for the points jersey, featured in Stage 20 breakaway. 20th GC.

name: Sakarias Koller Løland
bio: Young Norwegian sprinter. 9th on Stage 18.

name: Arjen Livyns
bio: Belgian breakaway specialist.

name: Harold Martín López
bio: Spanish climber in support role.

name: Matteo Malucelli
bio: Italian sprinter.

name: Christian Scaroni
bio: Italian climber and support rider.

name: Guillermo Thomas Silva
bio: Uruguayan puncheur. Won Stage 2 from a hilly breakaway. 6th on Stage 18.

name: Diego Ulissi
bio: Veteran Italian puncheur with a glittering career. Experienced leader of the team.
